needs_human_review.message: Test class 'StatePersistanceJSRootTest' is deleted but its test logic is moved to 'StatePersistenceTest' without verifying that the new test method covers the same scenarios. The deleted test used a server fixture with '--RegisterDynamicJSRootComponent' argument, which is not present in the new test.
needs_human_review.suggestion: Ensure the new test method 'PersistentStateIsSupportedInDynamicJSRoots' in 'StatePersistenceTest.cs' includes the necessary server fixture configuration (e.g., '--RegisterDynamicJSRootComponent') to replicate the original test's setup.

discarded.message: New module-level variable 'currentRendererId' is introduced but not used in the provided diff hunk. It may be unused or require additional initialization logic.
discarded.suggestion: Verify that 'currentRendererId' is used elsewhere in the file or remove it if unnecessary.

discarded.message: Missing curly braces around for-loop body may cause maintenance errors.
discarded.suggestion: Add curly braces around the inner for-loop body to prevent future bugs when adding statements.

discarded.message: Removing the conditional guard around RootComponents.RegisterForJavaScript changes behavior: the component is now always registered, even when the 'RegisterDynamicJSRootComponent' configuration flag is false. This may cause unintended registration in test scenarios that rely on the flag to control behavior.
discarded.suggestion: Restore the conditional check or ensure the test scenarios that depend on the flag are updated to expect the unconditional registration.

lines.content: // These are the public APIs at Blazor.rootComponents.*
// This will only happen in very nonstandard cases where someone has multiple hosts.
// It's up to the developer to ensure that only one of them enables dynamic root components.
// A different renderer type (e.g., Server vs WebAssembly) is trying to enable JS root components.
// This is a multi-host scenario which is not supported for dynamic root components.
throw new Error('Dynamic root components have already been enabled.');
// When the same renderer type re-enables (e.g., circuit restart or new circuit on same page),
// accept the new manager. The old manager's DotNetObjectReference is no longer valid anyway
// because the old circuit is gone. We don't dispose the old manager - doing so would cause
// JSDisconnectedException because the circuit that created it no longer exists.
// Call the registered initializers. This is an arbitrary subset of the JS component types that are registered
// on the .NET side - just those of them that require some JS-side initialization (e.g., to register them
// Call the registered initializers. This is an arbitrary subset of the JS component types that are registered
// on the .NET side - just those of them that require some JS-side initialization (e.g., to register them
// Licensed to the .NET Foundation under one or more agreements.
// The .NET Foundation licenses this file to you under the MIT license.
// These tests are for Blazor Web implementation
// For Blazor Server and Webassembly, check SaveStateTest.cs
// Clear local storage so that the resource hash is not found
